profile.name: Tal Shterzer
profile.summary: A hands-on systems architect and engineering manager with 17+ years building large-scale enterprise platforms and leading cloud-native modernization. Designs high-performance distributed systems on Azure, using well-known design patterns, containerization, and integrating AI coding agents into the development lifecycle. Builds and runs agile teams that ship secure, scalable products from architecture through code.

experience.title: Hands-On Development Manager & Cloud Architect
highlights: Led architecture and hands-on development of a healthcare workforce-management and payroll web platform.

Translated product and business requirements into architecture designs that are cloud-native, covering both functional and non-functional requirements such as performance, availability, security, and compliance.

Re-platformed a legacy monolith into a distributed-monolith architecture on Azure for 10,000 concurrent users, elastic to 35,000 and 300,000 active at peak, with SOC2 Type 2 PII protection across multi-tenant Azure SQL.
Led a team of cross-functioning 11 developers and devops engineers, owning hiring and mentoring, using daily agile rituals.
Helmed a BI project with a team of 4 data engineers and data scientists.
Introduced LLM coding agents for code reviews, testing and documentation, lifting sprint velocity by 50% for 2-week sprints.
Containerized services with Docker on Azure Container Apps.
Designed BI pipelining for ingesting into Microsoft Fabric and Apache Iceberg, utilizing Snowflake in a medallion architecture.
Modernized the system to include high-availability, strong resiliency and fault-tolerant services.
Hands-on experience with agentic AI coding tools such as Claude Code, Google Antigravity, and Windsurf.

achievements.text: Led architecture and hands-on development of a healthcare workforce-management and payroll web platform.

experience.title: Systems Architect & Senior Developer
highlights: Led migration of fintech systems for cloud-native architecture in .NET Core targeting scalability, and maintainability.
Helped to cut annual services cost from $2M to roughly $17K ($12K setup plus $400/month) by re-architecting a project onto Azure Blob Storage and Container Apps behind RESTFul API.
Led B2B projects enabling interfaces and reports between businesses, built on Microsoft Azure
Modernized WCF-based services with gRPC and Protocol Buffers, cutting network latency and payload size, enabling native streaming for terminal clients across Linux, macOS, and Windows.

experience.title: Hands-On Server-Side Team Lead
highlights: Managed a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) security product, including Business Intelligence solutions for large organizations on Microsoft SSAS
Led a 4-member development team using Agile-Scrum methodology
Developed and defined the system in .NET 4.0, utilizing WPF, WCF Services, and SQL Server 2008, to streamline security processes and improve customer service

experience.title: Hands-On Development Manager
highlights: Led a development project for the Ministry of Housing, managing a cross-functioning team of 8 (4 developers, 2 system analysts, 2 QA personnel), aiming to enhance the organizational information system
Designed and implemented solutions with .NET 3.5, BizTalk, and SQL Server, contributing to greater organizational efficiency
